metadata.name: Battle Cry — Signal Pest pumps Llanowar Elves but not itself
metadata.description: p1 controls Signal Pest (0/1, Battle Cry) and a Llanowar Elves (1/1). Both attack p2. Signal Pest's Battle Cry triggered ability fires when it attacks (CR 702.91a). After the trigger resolves, Llanowar Elves gets +1/+0 until end of turn and becomes 2/1. Signal Pest does NOT receive the bonus — the oracle text reads 'each OTHER attacking creature'. p2 declares no blockers. In the combat damage step, Llanowar Elves (power 2, unblocked) deals 2 damage to p2 and Signal Pest (power 0, unblocked) deals 0 damage. p2 life goes from 40 to 38. At cleanup (CR 514.2) the +1/+0 bonus expires and Llanowar Elves returns to 1/1.
metadata.generation_notes: Signal Pest oracle text (engine Available Cards list): 0/1 artifact creature, KeywordAbility::BattleCry. CR 702.91a: 'Whenever this creature attacks, each other attacking creature gets +1/+0 until end of turn.' The word 'other' is critical — Signal Pest itself does not benefit from its own trigger. Llanowar Elves base P/T 1/1; with the Battle Cry bonus becomes 2/1 until end of turn. Signal Pest base P/T 0/1; no change from the trigger. Combat damage (CR 510.1b): Llanowar Elves (power 2) deals 2 damage unblocked + Signal Pest (power 0) deals 0 damage = 2 total damage to p2. P2 life: 40 - 2 = 38. Turn 4 is used to avoid summoning sickness (CR 302.6). The declare_attackers action triggers Battle Cry, placing one triggered ability on the stack; both players pass priority; the trigger resolves granting Llanowar Elves +1/+0. Then declare_blockers step proceeds with no blockers declared. CR 514.2: At cleanup, the until-end-of-turn +1/+0 bonus expires, restoring Llanowar Elves to 1/1. No SBAs apply (Signal Pest takes no damage and has toughness 1, Llanowar Elves takes no damage and has toughness 1 enhanced by +0 = 1 toughness, p2 has 38 life).

script.step_note: p1 declares both Signal Pest and Llanowar Elves as attackers targeting p2. CR 508.1: both creatures are untapped and free of summoning sickness. CR 702.91a: Signal Pest's Battle Cry triggered ability fires — 'each other attacking creature gets +1/+0 until end of turn' — targeting Llanowar Elves. One trigger is placed on the stack.

actions.description: Initial: Signal Pest and Llanowar Elves untapped on p1 battlefield; life totals 40/40; stack empty
actions.note: Both creatures are untapped. Turn 4 — no summoning sickness (CR 302.6). Stack is empty before attackers are declared.

CR 508.1: p1 declares both Signal Pest and Llanowar Elves as attackers targeting p2. CR 508.1f: both creatures become tapped. CR 702.91a: Signal Pest's Battle Cry ability triggers — 'Whenever this creature attacks, each other attacking creature gets +1/+0 until end of turn.' One triggered ability is placed on the stack. Llanowar Elves is the only other attacking creature and will receive +1/+0 on resolution.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: both creatures tapped, one Battle Cry trigger on the stack
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Both attacking creatures are tapped. CR 702.91a: One Battle Cry triggered ability is on the stack.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority. The Battle Cry trigger (top of stack) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.91a: Battle Cry trigger resolves — Llanowar Elves (the only other attacking creature) gets +1/+0 until end of turn. Llanowar Elves is now 2/1. Signal Pest does NOT receive the bonus — 'each OTHER attacking creature'.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Battle Cry triggered ability resolves. Llanowar Elves gains +1/+0 until end of turn (becomes 2/1). Signal Pest remains 0/1 — it is not 'another' attacker from its own trigger.

actions.description: After Battle Cry resolves: stack empty, both creatures still on battlefield tapped
actions.note: Battle Cry trigger has resolved. Llanowar Elves has a +1/+0 until-end-of-turn bonus (effectively 2/1). Signal Pest remains 0/1.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in DeclareAttackers with empty stack — advance to DeclareBlockers step.

script.step_note: p2 controls no creatures and declares no blockers. Both Signal Pest and Llanowar Elves become unblocked attackers (CR 509.1h).

actions.note: CR 509.1: p2 declares no blockers (p2 controls no creatures). CR 509.1h: Both Signal Pest and Llanowar Elves become unblocked attackers.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to CombatDamage step. Llanowar Elves (power 2 with Battle Cry bonus) and Signal Pest (power 0) each deal damage to p2.

script.step_note: Combat damage turn-based action: Llanowar Elves (power 2, unblocked) assigns 2 damage to p2; Signal Pest (power 0, unblocked) assigns 0 damage to p2. Total: 2 damage. p2 life: 40 to 38.

actions.note: CR 704.5a: p2 has 38 life (above 0) — no player-death SBA. CR 704.5g: Signal Pest (0/1) and Llanowar Elves (1/1+0 toughness = 1) took no damage — no lethal-damage SBA. No SBAs apply.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after CombatDamage resolves — advance to EndOfCombat step.

actions.description: After combat damage: p2 at 38 life, both p1 creatures still on battlefield tapped
actions.note: CR 510.1b: Llanowar Elves (power 2 with Battle Cry +1/+0 bonus) dealt 2 combat damage to p2. Signal Pest (power 0) dealt 0 damage. Total: 2 damage. p2 life: 40 - 2 = 38. Both creatures remain on battlefield tapped. Llanowar Elves +1/+0 bonus persists until cleanup.

script.step_note: Cleanup step: all until-end-of-turn effects end simultaneously (CR 514.2). The Battle Cry +1/+0 bonus on Llanowar Elves expires, restoring it to 1/1. All damage marked on permanents is also removed.

actions.description: After cleanup — Battle Cry bonus expired: both p1 creatures on battlefield, p2 at 38 life
actions.note: CR 514.2: Until-end-of-turn effects end simultaneously in the cleanup step (turn-based action, does not use the stack). The Battle Cry +1/+0 bonus expires, restoring Llanowar Elves to its printed 1/1. Signal Pest remains 0/1. Both creatures remain on the battlefield (tapped). p2 retains 38 life.
